name: Andvaranaut
description: Andvaranaut, the Ring of Andvari, is a cursed ring in Norse mythology, forged by the dwarf Andvari. It possessed the power to generate gold, endlessly enriching its owner. Andvari crafted it to protect his hoard, but Loki, in payment for accidentally killing Ótr, was tasked by Odin and Hœnir to seize Andvari's gold. Loki captured Andvari and forced him to surrender all his wealth, including the ring. Andvari, in his rage and despair, cursed the ring, declaring that it would bring misfortune and death to whoever possessed it. This curse proved potent, as the ring played a significant role in the downfall of several characters in the Völsunga saga, including Fafnir and Sigurd. Fafnir, transformed into a dragon by the ring's corrupting influence, guarded the treasure until slain by Sigurd. Sigurd, despite knowing of the curse, took the ring, which ultimately led to his own demise. The ring's power to create gold is overshadowed by its devastating curse, representing the dangers of greed and the corrupting influence of wealth. It serves as a cautionary tale about the pursuit of riches at any cost and the inevitable consequences of ill-gotten gains. The Andvaranaut is a potent symbol of avarice and its destructive potential within the epic narratives of Norse mythology. The ring's legacy extends beyond its immediate impact on the Völsunga saga, serving as a motif for the destructive nature of unchecked ambition and the enduring power of curses in shaping destinies.
subtitle: The Cursed Ring of Endless Gold

keyMyths.title: Loki's Ransom
keyMyths.description: Loki, Odin, and Hœnir are forced to pay a wergild for accidentally killing Ótr. Loki captures Andvari and steals his gold, including the Andvaranaut, leading to the ring's curse.

keyMyths.title: Fafnir's Guard
keyMyths.description: Fafnir, driven by greed, slays his father Hreiðmarr to obtain the Andvaranaut and hoard. He transforms into a dragon and guards the treasure until slain by Sigurd.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A key source for Norse mythology, including the story of Loki's theft of Andvari's treasure.
sources.text: A key source for Norse mythology, including the story of Loki's theft of Andvari's treasure.

sources.author: Unknown
sources.description: An Icelandic saga that details the curse of the Andvaranaut and its impact on the Völsung clan.
